text: From my perspective, Forth is just a reminder that we’ve forsaken programming for an over-emphasis on automation of Production Engineering, automation of Premature Optimization and automation of the Waterfall Method. The word “programming” used to encompass a broad range of approaches, but, the word has been repurposed to mean a very narrow approach to solving problems. For example, the fundamental units in Forth are not “functions” and the ideas of parameter passing do not need to be hard-wired into hardware, McCarthy’s Lisp only showed how to build the scaffolding for the FP paradigm on top of reprogrammable electronic machines (in my opinion, Tunney’s Sector Lisp improves upon this by actually emphasizing FP).
